rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASOCIATION, INC. DBA GREAT VS. SAN MATEO CONVALESCENT HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA CORP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post Judgment Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 1, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASOCIATION, INC. DBA GREAT Motion To Compel Answers To Post Judgment Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: The plaintiff's motion is GRANTED. The defendants failed to respond to any of the interrogatories or requests for production, or ask for an extension of time to respond. All objections are waived. The defendants are to serve code compliant responses within thirty (30) days. The plaintiff's request for sanctions is GRANTED. Defendants shall pay plaintiff $360.00 within thirty (30) days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 1, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFFs ROBERT STEELE and ORLANDO NARANJO's Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement. Off calendar. Duplicate entry. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT STEELE VS. ZUMIEZ, INC., A WASHINGTON CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Final Approval Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 1, 2014, Line 4, Final Approval Hearing. Continued to October 31, 2014. Moving party shall file a supplemental brief with the Court no less than five court days prior to the new hearing date, addressing the following items: 1) the total number of participating class members; 2) the total number of participating class members in each subclass; 3) the aggregate number of workweeks of all class members; 4) the total number of workweeks of each subclass; 5) the number of weeks for which each individual class member qualifies; 6) the rate at which workweeks shall be reimbursed ("Workweek Value") for each subclass. The above should provide the formulas explaining the dollar values of each workweek for the two subclasses (e.g., "Class 1 is to be paid $70 per week, Class 2 is to be paid $90 per week."). Further, the disposition of uncashed checks leftover from the Net Settlement Amount should be clarified. The cy pres organization, the Zumiez Foundation, appears to be designated to receive uncashed settlement checks. The bona fides of the Zumiez Foundation should be explained. It appears to have close ties to the Defendant and the Court questions its benefits to a charitable cause-does it benefit Sumiez and its employees? Attorneys fees should be limited to 25%, thus 75% will be paid to the class less out of pocket expenses. Class counsel did not provide hourly rates or hours spent on this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VINCENT OTYANG VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summ Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 1, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication. Motion for summary judgment granted. As to the malicious prosecution claim, Defendant met its initial burden by providing evidence that Plaintiff was arrested by an officer in the scope of his employment. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 1-2.) Defendant is immune from liability per Gov. Code secs. 815.2 and 821.6. As to the violation of Civ. Code sec. 52.1 claim, Defendant met its initial burden by providing evidence that the officer had probable cause to arrest plaintiff and the officer did not use threats, intimidation, or coercion during the arrest. (UMF 1-21.) Plaintiff has not provided any admissible evidence in opposition and has therefore not raised a triable issue of material fact. Further, Plaintiff did not file an opposition to Defendant's separate statement in violation of CRC 3.1350(e). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY "SINTEZ" DIRECTLY AND VS. ALEXANDRE BAPA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 1, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT ALEXANDRE BAPA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to the Complaint is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action pursuant to C.C.P. § 430.10(e). A preliminary injunction is an interim remedy and is not, in itself, a cause of action (see MaJor v. Miraverde Homeowners Ass'n (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 618, 623) and Corporate Code § 1281.8(b) requires that an application for a Preliminary Injunction "be accompanied by a complaint or copies of the demand for arbitration and any response thereto." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PAULA HERTEL VS. AGESONG, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 1, 2014, Line 12, PLAINTIFF PAULA HERTEL's APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. Motion denied. Plaintiff has failed to identify the existence of a contract between the parties obligating Defendant's payment of any amount in deferred compensation, and has therefore failed to establish that the claim upon which the attachment is sought is one upon which an attachment may be issued. (CCP § 484.090(a)(1).) The December 2011 emails between Plaintiff and Defendant do not amount to a legally binding contract. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
